sections.paragraph:
const. פִּי, with suff. פִּי (my mouth), פִּיו ;פִּיךָ, פִּיהֶם ;פִּיהוּ, poet. פִּימֹו pl. פִּים 1Sa 13:21, and פִּיֹּות. Pro 5:4 (both of signif. 3).

(1) the mouth. (Arab. فُوهَةُ, فَاهُ, فِيهُ, فُوهُ, const. فا, فى, فو, Eth. አፋ፡, id. The Hebrew form פֶּה is for פֵּיֶה, like שֶׂה for שֵׂיֶה, whence the fem. פֵּיָה; const. פִּי for פּיִי. The root פָּיָה, like פָּהָה, פָּאָה, and Med. quiesc. פּוּא, פּוּהַּ, פּוּחַ, فَاهَ had the sense of blowing, breathing, and the mouth is so called from the idea of breathing. [In Thes. from פָּאָה.] It is used of the mouth of animals (and even of the beak of a bird, Gen 8:11 Isa 10:14); of men, Exo 4:11 especially as being the organ of speech. To speak פֶּה אֶל־פֶּה mouth to mouth, without any one between, Num 12:8 פֶּה אֶחָד with one mouth, with one voice, Jos 9:2. Job 19:16, בְּמֹו־פִי “with (all) my mouth”, i.e. with the loudest voice that I can. Meton. for an orator, spokesman, messenger, Exo 4:16 (comp. נָבִיא); commandment, Ecc 8:2, פִּי מֶלֶךְ שְׁמֹר “keep the commandment of the king.” Hence עַל פִּי by the command of, see below. More rarely the mouth as the organ of taste, hence Gen 25:28, “venison (was) בְּפִיו in his mouth”, i.e. he liked its taste. Comp. בְּפִי, כְּפִי. The signification of mouth is variously applied to inanimate things. It is-

(2) the mouth, opening of a bag, Gen 42:27 of a well, Gen 29:2 entrance of a city, Pro 8:3 of Hades, Psa 141:7.

(3) an edge (a figure taken from the teeth, and the idea of biting), especially in the phrase הִכָּה לְפִי חֶרֶב to smite with the edge of the sword (see נָכָה ). Plur. (see above) the edge (of instruments of iron), 1Sa 13:21 Pro 5:4. See פִּיפִיֹּות .

(4) edge, border (from its similarity to a lip), as of a garment, Psa 133:2 Job 30:18 of the sea, Pro 8:29. פֶּה לָפֶה 2Ki 10:21 21:16 and מִפֶּה אֶל־פֶּה Ezr 9:11, from one edge to the other.

(5) part, portion (prop. as much food as any one can take in his mouth, comp. יָד No. 7, a portion which can be taken in the hand, a handful), Deu 21:17, פִּי שְׁנַיִם “two parts”, i.e. double; 2Ki 2:9 also two (third) parts, Zec 13:8.

With prepositions it mostly becomes a particle-(l) כְּפִי-(a) according to the commandment of, 1Ch 12:23.-(b) according to the rate of (nach Ausfage), at the rate of, or proportion, according to, Lev 25:52, כְּפִי שָׁנָיו “according to his years;” Num 6:21 7:5 , 35:8 .-(c) i.q. כְּ, כְּמֹו like as, like, Job 33:6, אֲנִי כְפִיךָ לָאֵל “I, even as thou (am created) by God.”-(d) כְּפִי אֲשֶׁר conj. according as, even as, Mal 2:9 and without אֲשֶׁר so that, Zec 2:4.

(2) לְפִי i.q. כְּפִי (see לְ A, 9 )-(a) at the rate of, according to, Exo 12:4 Gen 47:12, לְפִי הַטָּף “according to the number of the family;” Hos 10:12, “sow ye according to justice, קִצְרוּ לְפִי חֶסֶד and ye shall reap according to mercy.”-(b) followed by an inf. when (i.q. לְ followed by an inf. No. 7), Num 9:17 Isa 29:10.

(3) עַל פִּי according to the commandment, order of any one, Gen 45:21 Num 3:16, 39 , 51 Job 39:27 by the authority of any one, Deu 17:6 hence i.q. כְּפִי, לְפִי at the rate of, according to, Amo 6:5 Pro 22:6. עַל־פִּי הַדְּבָרִים according to the thing itself, i.e. as the thing was, according to the truth of the matter, Gen 43:7.
